message:
Protect dirty blocks from cache eviction, add btrfs parent lookup fallback, and optimize FUSE I/O predictors

Block Cache (ffs-block):
- Make ARC eviction dirty-block-aware: scan for the first clean (non-dirty)
  block in the preferred queue (T1 or T2) via iter().position() instead of
  blindly popping the front. Fall back to the other queue if no clean block
  is found. This prevents evicting blocks with unflushed writes.
- Apply the same dirty-block protection to S3-FIFO rebalance: skip dirty blocks
  during T1/T2 rebalancing by pushing them to the back of their queue, with
  attempt counters to prevent infinite loops.

Btrfs Core (ffs-core):
- Add read-only fallback for parent directory (dotdot) resolution: when no COW
  allocator state is available, walk the btrfs filesystem tree for INODE_REF
  items matching the directory objectid and extract the parent from key.offset.
  This fixes ".." navigation in read-only btrfs mounts.

FUSE Layer (ffs-fuse):
- Add LRU BTreeMap to AccessPredictor for O(log n) eviction of oldest inode
  history entries, replacing the previous O(n) min_by_key scan.
- Fix coalesced read size to clamp rather than min, ensuring coalesced reads
  are never smaller than the requested size.
- Replace BTreeMap-only readahead buffer storage with ReadaheadState struct
  containing both a BTreeMap (key lookup) and VecDeque (FIFO insertion order),
  so eviction removes the oldest inserted entry rather than the smallest key.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
